steps.title: Use the dash clarification
steps.content: The narrator says novelty is not <u>essentially</u> new because it is only a timid variation on what already exists. The word therefore concerns something's basic nature, not its surface appearance, emotional tone, or frequency.

steps.title: Test the word meanings
steps.content: Choice A means only on the surface. Choice B means with mixed feelings. Choice D means often. The remaining choice means at the core or in basic nature, matching the narrator's claim about novelty. The answer is C.
